items.title: Anti-Compensatory Saccades Changes After Visuo-Vestibular Physical Therapy in People With Acute Unilateral Vestibulopathy: A Prospective Observational Study.
items.summary: This prospective observational study of 92 AUVP patients found that an 8-week visuo-vestibular physical therapy program improved anti-compensatory saccades (AcS) as assessed by the Suppression Head Impulse Paradigm (SHIMP), suggesting AcS may reflect central compensation.
items.curatedReason: 这项观察性研究提示，视听-前庭物理治疗可改善急性单侧前庭病变患者的反补偿性眼跳，表明 AcS 可能是评估前庭中枢代偿的一个生物标志物。在临床上，可将 SHIMP 作为跟踪 VVPT 效果的工具。
items.titleZh: 急性单侧前庭病变患者经视听-前庭物理治疗后反补偿性眼跳的变化：一项前瞻性观察性研究
items.summaryZh: 这项针对 92 名急性单侧前庭病变患者的前瞻性观察性研究发现，为期 8 周的视听-前庭物理治疗项目改善了通过抑制性头部冲击范式 (SHIMP) 评估的反补偿性眼跳，提示反补偿性眼跳可能反映了中枢代偿。
items.curatedReasonEn: This observational study suggests that visuo-vestibular physical therapy can improve anti-compensatory saccades (AcS) in patients with acute unilateral vestibulopathy, indicating that AcS may serve as a biomarker for central vestibular compensation. Clinically, you can use SHIMP to track the effects of VVPT.
items.limitation: 本研究为观察性研究，无法建立因果关系，且缺乏对照组来直接比较干预效果。
items.limitationEn: This was an observational study, which cannot establish causality, and lacked a control group for direct comparison of intervention effects.
items.journal: Physiotherapy research international : the journal for researchers and clinicians in physical therapy

items.title: Prioritising fall prevention strategies in Parkinson's disease: A multi-method evaluation.
items.summary: This multi-method study integrated a narrative review, a two-round Delphi process with 15 experts, and multi-criteria decision-making to identify and quantitatively prioritize fall-prevention strategies for people with Parkinson's disease.
items.curatedReason: 这项多方法评估为帕金森病患者的跌倒预防策略提供了量化优先级。你在临床实践中可以参考这些优先级，优化干预方案的资源分配，更有效地降低跌倒风险。
items.titleZh: 优先排序帕金森病跌倒预防策略：一项多方法评估
items.summaryZh: 这项多方法研究整合了叙述性综述、与 15 名专家进行的两轮德尔菲法以及多标准决策分析，旨在识别并量化优先排序帕金森病患者的跌倒预防策略。
items.curatedReasonEn: This multi-method evaluation provides a quantitative prioritization of fall prevention strategies for people with Parkinson's disease. You can use these priorities in clinical practice to optimize resource allocation for interventions, more effectively reducing fall risk.
items.limitation: 研究结果基于专家共识和决策分析，而非随机对照试验，可能受专家主观判断影响。
items.limitationEn: The study results are based on expert consensus and decision analysis, not randomized controlled trials, and may be influenced by expert subjective judgment.

items.title: Associations of neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing with rehabilitation outcomes in patients with stroke.
items.summary: This prospective observational study explored the associations of neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing with rehabilitation outcomes in stroke patients, finding potential links to poorer functional recovery and quality of life after a 4-week inpatient rehabilitation program.
items.curatedReason: 这项前瞻性观察性研究提示，卒中后的神经病理性疼痛和疼痛灾难化与较差的康复结局相关。这意味着在卒中康复中，你需要主动筛查并管理这类疼痛和心理因素，以期改善患者的长期功能恢复和生活质量。
items.titleZh: 神经病理性疼痛和疼痛灾难化与卒中患者康复结局的关联
items.summaryZh: 这项前瞻性观察性研究探讨了卒中患者神经病理性疼痛和疼痛灾难化与康复结局的关联，发现在为期 4 周的住院康复计划后，两者可能与较差的功能恢复和生活质量相关联。
items.curatedReasonEn: This prospective observational study suggests that neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing post-stroke are associated with poorer rehabilitation outcomes. This means you need to actively screen for and manage these pain and psychological factors in stroke rehabilitation to improve patients' long-term functional recovery and quality of life.
items.limitation: 本研究为观察性研究，无法确立神经病理性疼痛和疼痛灾难化与康复结局之间的因果关系。
items.limitationEn: This observational study cannot establish a causal relationship between neuropathic pain and pain catastrophizing and rehabilitation outcomes.

items.title: TRAK MSK: a randomised external feasibility trial and process evaluation of a digital physiotherapy supported self-management programme for people with musculoskeletal pain.
items.summary: This two-arm multi-site randomized external feasibility trial explored the feasibility of the TRAK MSK digital physiotherapy supported self-management program for musculoskeletal pain, recruiting adults with activity-related joint pain for at least 3 months.
items.curatedReason: TRAK MSK 数字项目在可行性上通过了验证。这意味着后续会有大规模 RCT 来检验其疗效。现在你可以关注这类数字工具的临床整合潜力，但不要急于将其作为常规干预。
items.titleZh: TRAK MSK：一项数字物理治疗支持的肌肉骨骼疼痛自我管理项目随机外部可行性试验和过程评估
items.summaryZh: 这项两臂多中心随机外部可行性试验，旨在探索 TRAK MSK 数字物理治疗支持的自我管理项目用于肌肉骨骼疼痛患者的可行性，招募了有至少 3 个月活动相关关节疼痛的成年人。
items.curatedReasonEn: The TRAK MSK digital program has demonstrated feasibility, paving the way for larger RCTs to evaluate its efficacy. You can now monitor the potential for integrating such digital tools into clinical practice, but do not rush to adopt it as a routine intervention.
items.limitation: 这是一项可行性试验，而非疗效试验，结果不能直接外推到临床疗效。
items.limitationEn: This is a feasibility trial, not an efficacy trial, so results cannot be directly extrapolated to clinical outcomes.

items.title: Major discrepancies in recommendations regarding long-term activity restrictions following knee replacement: a survey among Swedish physiotherapists.
items.summary: This survey among Swedish physiotherapists revealed major discrepancies in recommendations regarding long-term activity restrictions following knee replacement, indicating limited evidence and potential impact on patient recovery and fear of movement.
items.curatedReason: 膝关节置换术后长期活动限制缺乏共识，导致 PT 建议存在巨大差异。这提醒你，在给出建议时要基于现有证据，并向患者解释不确定性，以减少其运动恐惧。
items.titleZh: 膝关节置换术后长期活动限制建议存在重大差异：一项针对瑞典物理治疗师的调查
items.summaryZh: 这项针对瑞典物理治疗师的调查显示，膝关节置换术后长期活动限制的建议存在重大差异，表明现有证据有限，并可能影响患者的康复和运动恐惧。
items.curatedReasonEn: There is a significant lack of consensus on long-term activity restrictions post-knee replacement, leading to wide variations in PT recommendations. This highlights the importance of basing your advice on available evidence and discussing uncertainties with patients to mitigate kinesiophobia.
items.limitation: 研究对象仅限于瑞典的物理治疗师，结果的跨文化和跨地区外推性有限。
items.limitationEn: The study population was limited to Swedish physiotherapists, limiting cross-cultural and regional generalizability of the findings.

items.title: Early-Stage Symptomatic Hip Osteoarthritis: A Window of Opportunity.
items.summary: This commentary emphasizes the growing prevalence of hip osteoarthritis (OA) in young adults and argues that early-stage symptomatic hip OA presents a critical window for intervention to potentially slow or change its trajectory, an area often overlooked in research and clinical practice.
items.curatedReason: 这篇述评强调了早期阶段症状性髋关节骨关节炎的识别和干预的重要性。你应该关注年轻成人髋关节 OA 的早期筛查，并在临床上积极探索和应用早期非手术治疗策略，以期改变疾病进展轨迹。
items.titleZh: 早期症状性髋关节骨关节炎：一个干预的窗口期
items.summaryZh: 这篇述评强调了年轻成人髋关节骨关节炎（OA）日益增长的患病率，并指出早期症状性髋关节 OA 提供了一个关键的干预窗口，可能减缓或改变其病程，而这在研究和临床实践中常被忽视。
items.curatedReasonEn: This commentary highlights the importance of identifying and intervening in early-stage symptomatic hip osteoarthritis. You should focus on early screening for hip OA in young adults and actively explore and apply early non-surgical treatment strategies in clinical practice to potentially alter disease progression.
items.journal: The Journal of orthopaedic and sports physical therapy

items.title: Multi-Domain Benefits of Exergaming for Sarcopenia Management in Institutionalized Older Adults: Physical, Psychological, and Social Outcomes.
items.summary: A 12-week pragmatic quasi-experimental study involving 72 institutionalized older adults with sarcopenia demonstrated multi-domain benefits (physical, psychological, social) from an exergaming intervention (Nintendo Switch Ring Fit Adventure).
items.curatedReason: 为机构化老年肌少症患者提供电子游戏锻炼（exergaming）是个值得推的方案。这项准实验研究显示，12 周的任天堂 Switch Ring Fit Adventure 锻炼，在身体、心理和社会功能上都有显著改善。这为你在临床实践中引入互动式技术提供了强有力证据。
items.titleZh: 电子游戏锻炼对机构化老年肌少症患者的多领域益处：身体、心理和社会结局。
items.summaryZh: 一项为期 12 周的准实验研究，对 72 名机构化老年肌少症患者进行干预，结果显示，电子游戏锻炼（任天堂 Switch Ring Fit Adventure）在身体、心理和社会结局方面均带来了显著的多领域益处。
items.curatedReasonEn: Exergaming for institutionalized older adults with sarcopenia is a promising intervention. This quasi-experimental study shows 12 weeks of Nintendo Switch Ring Fit Adventure significantly improved physical, psychological, and social outcomes. This provides strong evidence for integrating interactive technology into your clinical practice.
items.limitation: 本研究为准实验设计，无法完全排除潜在混杂因素的影响。
items.limitationEn: As a pragmatic quasi-experimental study, it cannot completely exclude the influence of potential confounding factors.
items.journal: Archives of physical medicine and rehabilitation
